flowPackets.notes: Section 9 'Related Documentation' links to 3 module docs (auth, company, admin-portal-rbac). No ADR links — auth flow is foundational and no ADRs directly govern it. No frontend handoff link — auth is platform-level and not specific to a single frontend handoff packet.

Section 9 'Related Documentation' links to 3 module docs. No ADR links — tenant management is covered by core module docs. No frontend handoff link — could arguably link to frontend-handoff-platform.md but admin management spans all handoff docs.
Section 9 'Related Documentation' links to 3 module docs, 1 frontend handoff doc (operations), and deprecated index. No ADR links but this is acceptable as no ADRs directly govern catalog setup.

Section 10 'Related Documentation' links to 3 module docs. No ADR links — manufacturing is not directly governed by any ADR. No frontend handoff link — could arguably link to frontend-handoff-operations.md but the flow packet focuses on backend behavior.
Section 10 'Related Documentation' links to 4 module docs, 1 frontend handoff doc (operations), and deprecated index. No ADR links but inventory flow is not directly governed by any specific ADR.

Section 10 'Related Documentation' has all three link categories: 4 module docs, 1 ADR (ADR-006 portal/host boundary), and 1 frontend handoff (commercial). This is the most complete cross-linking example.

Section 10 'Related Documentation' has all three link categories: 3 module docs, 2 ADRs (ADR-003 outbox pattern, ADR-004 audit surfaces), and 1 frontend handoff (commercial). Plus a dedicated 'Relevant ADRs' subsection.

Section 9 'Related Documentation' links to 4 module docs. Has 'Relevant ADRs' subsection with ADR-002 and ADR-006. No frontend handoff link — invoice/dealer finance spans both commercial and finance handoff docs but neither is directly linked.

Section 10 'Related Documentation' links to 2 module docs and internal accounting docs directory. Has 'Relevant ADRs' subsection with ADR-004 and ADR-005. No frontend handoff link — accounting period close is backend-only, no specific frontend handoff doc covers it.

Section 10 'Related Documentation' has all three link categories: 2 module docs, 2 ADRs (ADR-002 auth scoping, ADR-004 audit surfaces), and 1 frontend handoff (finance). Plus deprecated index and historical workflow doc.

Section 10 'Related Documentation' links to 3 module docs and internal accounting flows doc. No ADR links — reporting is a consumer of other modules' data, not directly governed by ADRs. No frontend handoff link — reports are backend-driven with no specific frontend handoff coverage.

summary: PASS — All 11 flow packets contain a 'Related Documentation' section with links to owning module packets. 6 of 11 packets link to ADRs where applicable (order-to-cash, procure-to-pay, invoice-dealer-finance, accounting-period-close, hr-payroll have explicit ADR links; the remaining 5 have no ADRs that directly govern them, which is acceptable). 4 of 11 packets link to frontend handoff docs (catalog-setup-readiness→operations, inventory-management→operations, order-to-cash→commercial, hr-payroll→finance, procure-to-pay→commercial). The remaining 6 packets cover backend-only flows (auth, tenant-admin, manufacturing-packing, accounting-period-close, reporting-export) or span multiple handoff domains (invoice-dealer-finance). No broken links found — all referenced files exist on disk. The cross-linking satisfies the assertion that major flow packets link back to owning module docs, relevant ADRs, and related frontend handoff packets.
